meta.disclaimer: Booking windows are based on historical averages. Prices vary by airline, demand, and world events. Always compare prices across multiple dates.

routes.notes: LHR to CWB always connects via GRU (São Paulo Guarulhos). Flights are typically operated by LATAM, British Airways, or Air France/KLM. Book 6–8 weeks ahead for best fares. Avoid December and January when Brazilian domestic tourism pushes prices up sharply.
Direct route operated by British Airways and LATAM. Book 6–10 weeks ahead. March/April and September/October consistently cheapest.

Heavily competitive route with multiple airlines (BA, Virgin, American, United, Delta, Norse, Level). Prices can be very low with budget transatlantic carriers. Avoid summer and Christmas. Book 4–8 weeks ahead — last-minute prices are often reasonable on this route.
Usually routes via Gulf carriers (Emirates, Qatar, Etihad) or direct with Thai Airways or BA. Book 8–12 weeks ahead. Thai high season (Nov–Feb) commands premium prices.

Extremely competitive route. Emirates, flydubai, British Airways, and Virgin all fly this. Summer (when Dubai is 45°C) is the cheapest time to fly — many British visitors avoid the heat. Book 3–6 weeks ahead is usually fine. Christmas is very expensive.

One of the world's longest commercial routes. Qantas operates direct from LHR (via Perth). Gulf and Singapore Airlines via their hubs often cheapest. Book well in advance — 10–16 weeks. Australian Christmas (Dec/Jan) is peak.

Japan's cherry blossom season (late March–April) is extremely popular — book 14+ weeks ahead and expect premium prices. Golden Week (late April/early May) also very busy. February is often the cheapest month.
Competitive route with Air India, British Airways, Virgin, and Gulf carriers. Indian festival season (Oct/Nov) is peak. Monsoon months (July/August) are cheaper but weather can affect plans.

Mainly charter and TUI flights from Gatwick. Spring break (Feb/March) and summer are peak. May/June and September/October offer good value — still warm but hurricane season risk rises in September/October.
Kenya Airways and British Airways are main carriers. Peak is July/August (great migration) and December/January. Book well ahead for summer safari season.

regions.name: South America
regions.notes: Christmas and New Year are expensive across all South American destinations — Brazilian domestic tourism peaks then. European summer (July) also commands premiums. March/April and September/October are the sweet spot.

regions.name: North America
regions.notes: Transatlantic competition keeps prices reasonable. European summer is peak for North America too. January/February (post-Christmas) can be very good value. Mexico's Yucatán coast is cheapest in low season (May/June and September/October).

regions.name: Southeast Asia
regions.notes: Thai and regional high season is November–February (cooler, drier). European Christmas pushes prices up. May/June and September are cheap but can be rainy in some areas — research weather for your specific destination.

regions.name: East Asia
regions.notes: Japan cherry blossom season (late March–April) requires very early booking. Golden Week (late April/early May) also expensive. Chinese New Year disrupts many Asian destinations. February is often cheapest across the region.

regions.name: South Asia
regions.notes: India and Sri Lanka tourist season is October–March (cooler, drier). Monsoon (June–September) brings cheap prices but heavy rain. Maldives has no true off-season due to dry zones.

regions.name: Middle East
regions.notes: Extremely hot summers (June–August) mean rock-bottom prices. Dubai winter (December–March) is peak, especially Christmas/New Year. The route is highly competitive so last-minute deals are common.

regions.name: Sub-Saharan Africa
regions.notes: East Africa (Kenya, Tanzania) peaks in July/August for the Great Migration. Southern Africa peaks in European summer and Christmas. South Africa's Cape Town peaks December–February (their summer). Book well ahead for safari trips.

regions.name: Oceania
regions.notes: Australia and New Zealand are expensive long-haul routes. December–January is double peak — UK Christmas travellers and Australian/NZ summer. Book 10–16 weeks ahead minimum. February/March can offer significant savings.
